data.body: Landed (commit 1a37daa1). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 5 advisory — concrete filename, trace artifact form, scope all made concrete). The experiment did its job: built a realistic offline 4-pass skeleton (examples/review_skeleton.yaml: overlay_triggers -> batch finder over a prompt-library-backed criteria list with a conditional criterion -> aggregate verify -> decide) that runs end-to-end under FakeAgentRunner with ZERO live calls, and a reusable PlannedTraceRunner that captures the [prompt, intended-model, mode, call-mode, batched-criteria] trace OFFLINE — proving epic B's planned-trace parity mechanism is emittable from the engine. FLAWS SURFACED: (1) the linter skipped batch steps, so a batch's nested prompts went unvalidated — FIXED here (lint_prompt_refs now existence-checks batch.prompt + criteria[].prompt). (2) the plan-review criterion prompts are not in the workflow prompt catalog — RECORDED as a B prerequisite (commented on peak-fir-hilt; the criteria-library<->prompt-library wiring). Code-review subagent (general-purpose): clean — verified the trace captures the INTENDED (not echoed) model, the skeleton lints clean via the full linter, the linter fix preserves the agent path. Full suite 3251 passed/13 skipped (run ALONE, HEAD stable); make lint clean.
